latestCommit: see branch HEAD after final handoff commit

blockedPhases.reason: requires explicit production flag and live restoration proof before the approval flow and copied-config-tree proof can be wired to Apply
requires proven out-of-band recovery and live restoration proof before production writes
requires trusted 0.55.4 export/source evidence before app model migration

nextExactPhaseToContinue: Define the future explicit user-approval UX and live production dry-run policy without wiring source/include or duplicate production executors.
testsPassing: cargo fmt, cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo test, jq empty data/reports/*.json, git diff --check, cargo build --release, and GTK safe-env matrix passed after activation draft-edit review updates.
validationStatus: passed after activation draft-edit review updates
knownRisks: No real live/system mutation was run in this sprint; approval-card work was report-backed UI/projection and GTK safe-env assertion work only.
Sandboxed read-only hyprctl and direct socket probes fail because the sandbox cannot connect to the Hyprland Unix socket; outside-sandbox read-only hyprctl succeeds.

Controlled general:gaps_in live restore proof succeeded with hl.config eval syntax after keyword and assignment eval failed before value change; that proof now feeds an approved_but_default_disabled runtime approval review and disabled UI evidence surface while production runtime/reload remains disabled.
Source/include insertion remains production-disabled beyond single-root scope; the deep disabled approval card is review-only and has no production handler.
Deep approval card data now loads through the serialized report-backed adapter, with Missing from report fallback copy for incomplete records.
High-risk/display mutation remains blocked until out-of-band recovery can be proven before any mutation.
0.55.4 migration remains blocked without trusted official exports and row/write-safety review.
Activation decision reviews are review-only; production flags remain false and no production executor is wired.
Activation path reviews remain review-only; production activation flags and executors are still false/unwired.
Activation control reviews validate complete request and safety-plan inputs for source/include and duplicate, but require executors to remain Unwired and keep all production flags false.

Activation form/state-machine reviews collect request and safety-plan field data for source/include and duplicate, validate through the activation controls, and remain review-only with executors Unwired.
Activation form fields now render as real disabled GTK Entry/TextView/CheckButton widgets with stable names and screenshot/accessibility field-label assertions.

Activation draft state now updates/resets source/include and duplicate form values in memory only, validates through the existing form/control pipeline, displays disabled draft cards, and adds no persistence or executor wiring.

Activation draft-edit review models in-memory-only edit mode, and the live GTK draft edit bridge now updates memory-only draft state from field edits, recomputes validation, resets in memory, and keeps executors Unwired.

Activation draft persistence now has a default-disabled boundary: persistence is forbidden by default, no draft data is written to disk, storage path is none, no serializer/write path exists, executors remain Unwired, and production flags remain false.

Production activation safety gates now exist for source/include and duplicate. Copied-fixture proof satisfies byte-exact backup, write/reread/restore, post-restore verification, rollback, no-auto-apply, persisted-draft auto-apply, and report-backed proof, but final approval, production flag, executor wiring, and live production dry-run decisions remain required.

Production activation final decision reviews now explicitly keep final approval, production flag opt-in, executor wiring opt-in, and live production dry-run policy missing/required; copied-fixture proof cannot approve production, set flags, wire executors, or authorize live dry-runs.

The remaining dependency scan classifies all tracker categories; no additional independent category was uniquely safe to implement after the draft-edit layer because remaining work is capped or blocked by production activation, high-risk recovery proof, or missing official 0.55.4 data.
recommendedNextCodexPrompt: Define the future explicit user-approval UX and live production dry-run policy without wiring source/include or duplicate production executors.
summary: Source/include and duplicate activation decisions now consume report-backed approval card data and remain ApprovedButDefaultDisabled.
Source/include and duplicate activation paths consume approved-but-default-disabled decisions and report-backed proof, then stop at explicit production flag false.
Source/include and duplicate activation controls validate complete review-only request and safety-plan inputs, prove executors remain unwired, and keep production disabled.

Source/include and duplicate activation forms collect request and safety-plan field data, generate review-only request/plan values, validate through the controls as ValidatedButExecutorUnwired, and keep production disabled.
Source/include and duplicate activation form values now render through real disabled GTK form fields while executors remain Unwired.
Source/include and duplicate activation draft state can update/reset values in memory only and validates through the form/control pipeline as review-only.

Source/include and duplicate activation live GTK draft edit bridges update memory-only draft state from field edits, recompute form/control validation, reset in memory, and keep persistence absent with executors Unwired.

Source/include and duplicate activation draft persistence boundaries forbid persistence by default, expose no storage path, write no draft data to disk, call no serializer, and list the future opt-in/storage/redaction/retention/delete/encryption requirements.

Source/include and duplicate production activation safety proof is visible and partially satisfied by copied fixtures; final approval, production flag, executor wiring, and live production dry-run decisions remain required.

The remaining dependency scan found no single safe independent category to advance beyond the draft-edit work; remaining tracks are capped or blocked by explicit production activation, high-risk recovery proof, or trusted 0.55.4 export data.
